subject:
warp_tools 2D Particle Series and Relion5: reconstruct vs initial
 model
body:
*Dear All,*

This question not only concerns warp_tools but more generally 2D particle 
series and WARP + relion 5, but I hope somebody has a suggestion for me 😇

In our particular case, we are running relion 5.0-beta-0-commit-84ba16 and 
warp_tools on linux.

We use a python version of TOMOMAN to export TS to warp_tools and pytom_GPU 
for template matching.

We then extract the particle series in warp_tools.

When I generate an initial model from the 2D particle series, everything 
looks fine and I can run a refinement afterwards. [a bit annoying that the 
particle pose can change relative to the TM reference, but OK ... 😉]

However, having to run an initial model just to check if particle picks / 
particles are good is a bit cumbersome ...

>From the traditional (3d subtomo) workflow, I would just use 
relion_reconstruct to get the initial reference.

However, when trying to use relion_tomo_reconstruct_particle on my 
optimization set (the initial one from warp_tools), I only get empty 
volumes.

I suppose this is bc warp_tools will only link a "dummy_tiltseries.mrc"?

Is there a way to actually link the correct data in a relion-v5 approved 
way?


Are there any other options in v5 + warp_tools to reconstruct the particle 
series directly (i.e. without alignment)? [as relion reconstruct does not 
work on 2D particle stacks]

Thanks!
